identity.reason: https://www.agibot.com/products/A2 and https://agibot.com/jp/products/A2 are live, fully-specified current pages (169cm/69kg/700Wh/40+ active DoF/2h). The 'AGIBOT A2' nav entry itself has no href (it is a category header; only A2 Ultra/A2 Lite/A2-W are directly hyperlinked from the visible nav), so A2 is not featured in the primary marketing carousel. However there is no discontinuation/successor notice, and two official news articles dated 2025-12-02 and 2026-01-19 (within the last ~7 months) still market A2 by name and state AgiBot 'achieved large-scale delivery of more than 1,000 units in 2025, ranking first in the world in the full-size bipedal humanoid field' (attributed to A2 specifically by an AgiBot SVP). Treated as current, not historical, per the instruction not to assume discontinuation just because a product is absent from the top nav links.

procurement.note: Store shows schema.org availability=InStock but price ($999,999.00 USD) is a placeholder like A2 Ultra's — not a real usable price; real transactions require sales contact.

conflicts.detail: EN/store max 1.8m/s vs JP official page max 1.5m/s.

unresolved: Store display price is a non-functional placeholder identical in form to A2 Ultra's ($999,999.00).

identity.reason: Directly linked from current nav (/products/G2), and a very recent official press release (2026-06-28) states AGIBOT's 15,000th robot to roll off the production line 'is the AGIBOT G2, an industrial-grade embodied task robot designed for industrial and real-world operational scenarios,' following prior 5,000th/10,000th unit milestones — strong current mass-production evidence dated within the last month of the checkedAt date.

documentsReviewed.title: AGIBOT and Longcheer precision manufacturing deployment
AGIBOT global livestream of humanoid robots on a real factory production line
AGIBOT's 15,000th Robot Rolls Off the Production Line

evidence.evidenceSummary: Official article (dated March 2, 2026) describes G2 as 'wheeled humanoids optimized for industrial precision assembly and continuous operations.'
'Full Machine Protection: IP42'; confirmed identically on the JP page ('IP42対応').

currentFeatures.feature: 100% automotive-grade components
currentFeatures.evidenceSummary: '100% Automotive-Grade Components.'

currentFeatures.feature: Sub-millimeter precision assembly, force-control operation
currentFeatures.evidenceSummary: 'Sub-Millimeter Precision Assembly'; 'Force-Control Operation.'

currentFeatures.feature: Rapid deployment with Genie RL (reinforcement-learning platform)
currentFeatures.evidenceSummary: 'Rapid Deployment with Genie RL.'

currentFeatures.feature: Expressive facial animations, multi-user conversation, eye-gaze tracking
currentFeatures.evidenceSummary: Listed under 'Interactive Intelligence' features.

currentFeatures.feature: ~100 cumulative hours of live factory-floor operation (quality inspection)
currentFeatures.evidenceSummary: 'In late June, AGIBOT completed approximately 100 cumulative hours of factory livestream operations featuring the AGIBOT G2... operated across a tablet mass-production quality inspection section, aligned with factory production rhythms.'

officialUseCases.officialExpression: high-precision force-control, sub-millimeter assembly; loading/unloading and testing-fixture placement at MMIT stations
officialUseCases.reason: Both the product page's force-control/precision-assembly claim and the Longcheer deployment article's description of loading tablets into testing fixtures align with assembly-support work.

useCaseGaps.officialExpression: wheeled industrial precision assembly / continuous 24h operation, quality inspection at consumer-electronics production lines
useCaseGaps.reason: No existing UseCase specifically covers wheeled mobile-manipulator quality-inspection/loading work on continuous production lines at this granularity.

usageExamples.scopeNote: 2026-04-14 article: 'multiple AGIBOT G2 robots have been officially integrated into Longcheer's tablet production lines, working alongside human operators in real manufacturing environments,' performing MMIT-station loading/unloading, 310 units/hour, >99% success rate, 3,000 units/shift, 140+ cumulative hours at time of writing.

usageExamples.customerOrInstitution: AGIBOT company-wide production milestone naming G2 as the flagship unit
usageExamples.scopeNote: 2026-06-28 press release: AGIBOT's 15,000th robot overall was 'the AGIBOT G2'; G2 completed ~100 cumulative hours of livestreamed factory quality-inspection operation at the same Longcheer line.

usageExamples.variant: G2 (robot model not explicitly named in this article; inferred from same Longcheer/tablet-QC context as articles 60 and 82)
usageExamples.scopeNote: 2026-06 six-day global livestream (June 23-28) of 'multiple humanoid robots operating on a real factory production line' at the same Longcheer Nanchang tablet quality-inspection section; treated as supplementary since the model name is not stated explicitly in this article.

procurement.note: No store listing or public price; contact-only (sales@agibot.com). Despite this, official 2026-06-28 press material confirms G2 as AGIBOT's highest-volume current product (named as the 15,000th unit produced) and names a specific, repeatedly-confirmed commercial customer (Longcheer).

unresolved: No official numeric specs (height/weight/DoF/speed/payload/battery/compute) found on either the EN or JP official product page despite extensive checking; third-party spec-aggregator sites (humanoid.guide, originofbots.com, robotsinternational.com, etc.) publish numbers (e.g. 175cm/55kg, 26-49 DoF, Jetson Thor T5000) but per the research method these were treated as discovery-only and NOT adopted, since they conflict with each other and cannot be confirmed against any AgiBot-published page.
humanReviewRequired: AgiBot's own Document Center likely holds a full G2 datasheet; it was not reachable via the fetch tools used in this pass and should be checked directly (requires site login/navigation) in a follow-up.
